subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer 4 questions. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote theorems (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problems.text: Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with \(1 \ne 0\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an ideal of \(R\) to be maximal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that every proper ideal of \(R\) is contained in a maximal ideal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the polynomial ring \(k[x]\), where \(k\) is a field, is a Euclidean domain.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the definition of the term unique factorization domain.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that the ring \(\mathbb{Z}[\sqrt{-5}]\) is not a unique factorization domain.

problems.text: Let \(k\) be a field, let \(k[x,y]\) denote the polynomial ring in two variables over \(k\). Let \(R=k[x,y]/(xy)\) and denote the image in \(R\) of a polynomial \(f\in k[x,y]\) by \(\bar f\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(P\) is a prime ideal of \(R\) then either \(\bar x\in P\) or \(\bar y\in P\) (or both).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Describe all of the prime ideals of \(R\) that contain \(\bar y\).

problems.text: Using the rational canonical form, find one representative for each conjugacy class in \(\operatorname{GL}(5,\mathbb{F}_2)\) whose elements have order \(4\).
Let \(L\) be an algebraic field extension of the field \(K\) and let \(R\) be a subring of \(L\) that contains \(K\). Prove that \(R\) is a field.
